Khmer Rouge.description: The Khmer Rouge was a communist-led insurgent and later ruling party in Cambodia, founded in 1951 and led by Pol Pot. It ruled Cambodia from 1975 to 1979, implementing radical social engineering that resulted in the Cambodian genocide, with 1.5 to 2 million deaths. The group was defeated in 1979 but continued as an insurgency until 1999.

Clan Mariano.description: Italian Camorra criminal organization originating from the Quartieri Spagnoli in Naples. Founded in the 1980s by brothers Salvatore, Ciro, and Marco Mariano, former associates of the Giuliano brothers. It fought wars with the Faiano clan and the Secondigliano Alliance for control of the Quartieri Spagnoli.

edges.evidence_quote: L'origine della faida, secondo la ricostruzione dei Magistrati, risalirebbe al 24 marzo del 1991, Domenica delle Palme , in un agguato operato da Luca Mariani e da suo cugino Simone Festa, entrambi affiliati agli scissionisti Cardillo-Ranieri, nel tentativo di uccidere Giovanni Sole (allora considerato il braccio destro di Ciro Mariano), ma riuscirono a colpire a morte solo il suo autista, Ciro Napoletano.

Marielitos.description: Marielitos are Cuban immigrants who arrived in the United States during the 1980 Mariel boatlift, with a subset involved in organized crime. They are based in the United States, primarily Miami, and are known for drug and arms trafficking, gambling, and contract killing. The group has been active since the early 1980s and maintains alliances with other criminal organizations.

Barrio Azteca.description: Mexican-American street and prison gang originally based in El Paso, Texas and Ciudad Juarez, Mexico, founded in the early 1980s by Jose 'Raulio' Rivera. It is a transnational criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, human trafficking, murder, and other crimes, with an estimated 8,000 members. The gang has an alliance with La Línea and the Juárez Cartel, and is considered one of the most violent gangs in the United States.

Juárez Cartel.description: Mexican drug cartel based in Ciudad Juárez, Chihuahua, founded around 1970 by Rafael Aguilar Guajardo, Pablo Acosta Villarreal, Amado Carrillo Fuentes, and Vicente Carrillo Fuentes. It controls cocaine trafficking routes into the United States and is known for extreme violence, including decapitations. Its armed wing is La Línea, and it uses Barrio Azteca gang. Currently led by Juan Pablo Ledezma.

Mexican Mafia.description: The Mexican Mafia, also known as La eMe, is a powerful criminal organization based in the United States, primarily composed of individuals of Mexican origin. It was founded on November 15, 1957, by Luis "Huero Buff" Flores and 13 other Chicano gang members at the Deuel Vocational Institution in Tracy, California. The organization is one of the oldest and most powerful prison gangs in the U.S., controlling illegal activities within the prison system and on the streets. It operates mainly in California and other states, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, and murder.
